reforms.description: The government took measures to revitalize the KOSDAQ market and improve the KONEX market to facilitate early-stage IPOs for startups, aiming to enhance access to capital for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to capital for startups can stimulate innovation and economic growth by enabling new firms to enter the market and scale their operations.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively worked on measures to improve the KOSDAQ and KONEX markets.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the startup ecosystem, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism in Korea.
reforms.source_quote: The government took measures to revitalize the KOSDAQ market in January 2018 and improve the KONEX market in April 2017.

reforms.description: The government expanded the M&A component of the Growth Ladder Fund to support mergers and acquisitions of SMEs, allowing strategic investors to participate in special purpose companies established by private equity funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating M&A can enhance the competitiveness of SMEs, allowing them to scale and innovate more effectively.
reforms.status_evidence: The government expanded the size of the M&A component in the Growth Ladder Fund in 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it enhances the capacity of SMEs to engage in M&A, which is vital for their growth and innovation potential.
reforms.source_quote: The government expanded the size of the M&A component in the Growth Ladder Fund in 2017.

reforms.description: The Korean government aims to build an innovative ecosystem to foster SMEs, helping them to lead the fourth industrial revolution and increase their participation in world trade, as reflected in the creation of the Ministry of SMEs and Start-ups in 2017.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering an innovative ecosystem for SMEs, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and job creation, contributing to inclusive growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of SMEs and Start-ups was created to support this initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated ministry represents a structural change in the approach to SME support, aiming to significantly enhance their role in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The longstanding government objective of promoting growth through innovation requires a greater contribution from SMEs.

reforms.description: The government is launching a KRW10 trillion (USD9.3 billion) venture capital investment fund to boost financing for SMEs and start-ups, aiming to enhance their role in job creation and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases access to capital for innovative firms, fostering entrepreneurship and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is launching a KRW10 trillion venture capital investment fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This substantial investment represents a significant shift in policy to support the growth of SMEs and start-ups, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The government is launching a KRW10 trillion (USD9.3 billion) venture capital investment fund.

reforms.description: The Ministry of SMEs and Start-ups (MSS) is implementing all of its start-up programmes as 'Tech Incubator Programmes for Start-ups', which provide mentoring and technology development support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the capacity of start-ups to innovate and grow through targeted support and resources.
reforms.status_evidence: MSS is implementing all of its start-up programmes as 'Tech Incubator Programmes for Start-ups'.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms for start-ups, focusing on technology and mentorship.
reforms.source_quote: MSS is implementing all of its start-up programmes as 'Tech Incubator Programmes for Start-ups'.

reforms.description: The Korean government announced plans to assist 20,000 SMEs in establishing smart factories by 2022, integrating software and the Internet of Things to enhance productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to modernize SMEs, improving their productivity and competitiveness in the market through advanced technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced that it would help 20,000 SMEs establish smart factories by 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards integrating advanced technologies in SMEs, which is crucial for enhancing their productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced that it would help 20,000 SMEs establish smart factories, which integrates software and the Internet of Things, by 2022.

reforms.description: The Korean government provides tax incentives for R&D, asset acquisition, technology commercialization, and human resource support to promote innovation among SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives are designed to stimulate R&D investment, which can enhance productivity and innovation in SMEs, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has been providing these tax incentives to support R&D activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax incentives represent a significant effort to enhance R&D investment among SMEs, which is crucial for their growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: To promote innovation, the government provides tax incentives for R&D, asset acquisition, technology commercialisation and human resource support.

reforms.description: The government has developed a plan to promote international cooperation in science, technology, and innovation, emphasizing the creation of a global network of overseas science and technology outposts.
reforms.package_name: 2018 International Science and Technology Cooperation Plan
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing international cooperation can facilitate knowledge transfer and access to global innovation networks, which can drive domestic innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has developed a plan to promote such cooperation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant strategic shift towards enhancing international collaboration in science and technology, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has developed a plan to promote such cooperation, emphasising the creation of a global network of overseas science, technology and innovation outposts.

reforms.description: The Korean government aims to raise the share of public R&D spending in services from 3.4% to 6% over the next five years to enhance innovation in the service sector, which currently has the lowest R&D investment among OECD countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding in services is expected to boost productivity and innovation in a sector that is currently underperforming.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to raise the share to 6% during the next five years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities towards the service sector, which is crucial for enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to raise the share to 6% during the next five years.

reforms.description: The Korean government launched a plan in November 2017 to create an ecosystem to nurture innovative start-ups, focusing on fostering entrepreneurship and supporting diverse start-ups through various initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a supportive environment for start-ups, the plan aims to enhance innovation and entrepreneurship, which are vital for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched a plan in November 2017 to create an ecosystem to nurture innovative start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This comprehensive plan represents a structural change in how the government supports start-ups, aiming to create a vibrant ecosystem that can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched a plan in November 2017 to create an ecosystem to nurture innovative start-ups.

reforms.description: The government plans to transform the venture start-up support system into a private sector-driven model, utilizing the Tech Incubator Programme for Start-ups (TIPS) to enhance support during the seed stage of start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a private sector-driven support system, the reform aims to enhance the sustainability and growth potential of start-ups, thereby contributing to economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to transform the venture start-up support system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards a more sustainable model for start-up support, which is crucial for enhancing the entrepreneurial ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Transform the venture start-up support system into a private sector-driven system.

reforms.description: The government is establishing an 'innovation adventure fund' worth KRW 10 trillion to provide comprehensive funding for venture businesses throughout different stages, from commercialization to market launch.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This fund aims to enhance access to capital for innovative ventures, thereby supporting their growth and contribution to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Set up an 'innovation adventure fund' worth KRW 10 trillion.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a large-scale fund represents a major commitment to enhancing the financial ecosystem for innovation and start-ups.
reforms.source_quote: Set up an 'innovation adventure fund' worth KRW 10 trillion.

reforms.description: The government launched the Tech Incubator Programme for Start-ups (TIPS) in 2014 to better combine technological development and business expertise, providing R&D funding and mentoring for start-ups led by private-sector angel investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering start-ups with R&D funding and mentorship, TIPS aims to enhance innovation and job creation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been operational since its launch in 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: TIPS represents a significant shift in the approach to supporting start-ups, integrating R&D funding with private investment to enhance innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched the TIPS programme in 2014 to better combine technological development and business expertise.

reforms.description: A 2016 reform allows crowdfunding in the form of securities and loans for start-ups, enabling them to raise funds from non-professional individual investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing alternative financing options, the reform enhances access to capital for start-ups, promoting innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been in effect since 2016, allowing crowdfunding for start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform introduces a new financing mechanism for start-ups, which can significantly impact their growth potential and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: A 2016 reform allows crowdfunding in the form of securities and loans.

reforms.description: The R&D tax credit will be made more advantageous for small firms to encourage their investment in research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the R&D tax credit is expected to incentivize small firms to invest more in innovation, thereby boosting productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credit will be made more advantageous for small firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives aimed at enhancing innovation among small firms, which is crucial for their growth.
reforms.source_quote: Make the R&D tax credit more advantageous for small firms.
